display.purpose: Rare dashboard totals and major figures.

headline.purpose: Screen titles and dashboard page headers.

title.purpose: Card, section, drawer, and mobile top-bar titles.

body.purpose: Forms, reports, help copy, and default content.

label.purpose: Buttons, filters, form labels, table headers, and nav labels.

shadows.name: control
shadows.purpose: Subtle controls and low emphasis buttons.

shadows.name: card
shadows.purpose: Dashboard cards and table wrappers when borders need support.

shadows.name: card-md
shadows.purpose: Hover lift and raised dashboard surfaces.

shadows.name: card-lg
shadows.purpose: Floating nav controls, drawers, and high priority overlays.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, and active state transitions.

motion.name: view-transition
motion.purpose: Reduced, stateful page view transitions only when supported.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary operational action with Field Teal and visible focus.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Quiet alternative action for filters, navigation, and non-primary commands.

components.name: Text Field
components.description: RTL field with token border, clear focus, and Arabic readable sizing.

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Compact status label with dot and semantic tone.

components.name: Dashboard Card
components.description: Border-first dashboard surface for totals, forms, and report panels.

components.name: Dashboard Nav Item
components.description: RTL sidebar item with active state and compact icon slot.

narrative.overview:
EcoPest is a restrained operations interface for pest-control teams. The design should feel like a reliable field ledger brought into a modern dashboard: status-heavy, audit-aware, readable in Arabic, and quick to scan when the user is moving between stations, reports, shifts, clients, and reviews.

The default scene is practical rather than atmospheric: a technician using a phone outdoors or in a service corridor, and a supervisor or manager reading a dense dashboard at a desk. This forces a restrained theme with strong contrast, clear status, familiar controls, and no decorative spectacle. The product is allowed to be dense because the work is dense; it is not allowed to be vague.
keyCharacteristics: RTL-first layout with Arabic as the default reading direction.
Restrained color: neutral surfaces, teal for primary actions, semantic color for status.
Mobile field controls with large targets and explicit recovery states.
Complete light and dark themes through CSS variables.
Familiar product UI patterns over invented affordances.

rules.name: The Teal Means Action Rule
rules.body: Teal is for primary action, current selection, focus, and high-value operational emphasis. It is not decoration.

rules.name: The Status Must Survive Color Loss Rule
rules.body: Every status color needs a label, icon, dot, or placement cue. Never rely on color alone.

rules.name: The No Decorative Gradient Rule
rules.body: Gradients are forbidden for decoration, text, cards, heroes, and empty states. Existing technical overlays may remain only when they support surface depth or nav legibility.

rules.name: The Arabic Legibility Rule
rules.body: Field forms and mobile report screens use at least 16px for labels and inputs. Dense desktop tables may use 14px only when hierarchy remains clear.

rules.name: The No Display Font In Controls Rule
rules.body: Buttons, labels, table headers, badges, and data never use display styling.

rules.name: The Border First Rule
rules.body: Start with a border and a surface token. Add shadow only when the surface floats, responds to hover, or must separate from dense content.

rules.name: The No Heavy Card Rule
rules.body: Cards do not use theatrical shadows. If a card looks like a marketing tile, reduce shadow and tighten the content.

dos: Do keep `dir="rtl"` and Arabic-first layout on all primary web and mobile surfaces.
Do use Cairo for web UI and Tajawal for the current Expo mobile UI until the mobile typography system is intentionally changed.
Do use CSS variables and Tailwind tokens for colors; avoid hardcoded colors in new UI.
Do keep primary actions teal and status meaning semantic.
Do use `ps-`, `pe-`, `ms-`, and `me-` logical utilities for RTL spacing.
Do preserve large touch targets for technician and mobile companion workflows.
Do provide loading, empty, error, disabled, pending, sync, and review states for task-critical surfaces.
Do make AI or analytics output actionable, sourced, and bounded by available operational data.
donts: Don't create empty marketing hero sections.
Don't show vague AI outputs without actions.
Don't use generic SaaS gradients, gradient text, glass defaults, or purple-blue launch-page styling.
Don't repeat icon-heading-text grids as filler.
Don't use playful motion, animated spectacle, or custom affordances that slow standard admin work.
Don't use `text-black`, `#000`, or new hardcoded pure-white surfaces. Use tokens.
Don't use modals as the first answer when inline review, progressive disclosure, or page-level flows would work better.
